data.title: Brainstorm asks user questions answerable by codebase inspection
data.description:
During a brainstorm session, the skill asked the user a question that should have been answered by reading the codebase instead.

Offending question asked during brainstorm:
'Today, what happens when an orchestrator (sprint or fix-bug) tries to figure out where work was done on a ticket — meaning, which branch or worktree holds the in-progress changes? Does it look at git log, ticket comments, some state file, or does it essentially have no reliable way to know?'

This question is fully answerable by reading the sprint and fix-bug SKILL.md files and the worktree dispatch/harvest scripts. The brainstorm skill's Phase 1 Step 1 instructs: 'Investigate before asking: Before presenting any question to the user, check whether the answer is discoverable by reading the codebase.' The skill failed to apply this rule before presenting the question.

Expected behavior: Brainstorm reads sprint SKILL.md, fix-bug SKILL.md, and relevant worktree/harvest scripts to answer structural questions about the existing system before presenting them to the user.
